message:
Add PyObject print support, slicing, and dict literals

Three major features implemented by parallel agents:

Agent 1: Fix print() for PyObjects
- Updated visitPrintCall() in builtins.zig to detect PyObject variables
- Added switch statement to extract values based on type_id
- Updated visitEnumerateFor() and visitZipFor() to mark variables as "pyobject" type
- Now prints actual values instead of struct dumps for enumerate/zip results

Agent 2: Add Slicing Support
- Extended AST with Subscript, Slice, and SliceRange nodes
- Added subscript/slicing parser in parser.zig (supports [0], [1:3], [-1], [::2])
- Implemented visitSubscript() in expressions.zig
- Added runtime methods:
  - PyList: get(), slice(), sliceWithStep()
  - PyString: charAt(), slice(), sliceWithStep()
- Supports: simple indexing, negative indices, slice ranges, steps

Agent 3: Add Dict Literals and Methods
- Extended AST with Dict node type
- Added dict literal parsing {key: value, ...}
- Implemented visitDict() in expressions.zig
- Added dict subscripting: dict["key"]
- Added dict method calls: keys(), values()
- Added len() support for dicts
- Runtime detection for dict usage

Changes:
- src/ast.zig: Added Subscript and Dict node types
- src/parser.zig: Added subscript and dict literal parsing
- src/codegen/expressions.zig: Added visitSubscript() and visitDict()
- src/codegen/builtins.zig: Enhanced print() for PyObjects, added dict len()
- src/codegen/control_flow.zig: Mark enumerate/zip vars as "pyobject"
- src/codegen/classes.zig: Added dict.keys() and dict.values() methods
- src/codegen/statements.zig: Track dict variable types
- packages/runtime/src/pylist.zig: Added get(), slice() methods
- packages/runtime/src/pystring.zig: Added charAt(), slice() methods

Build Status: ✅ Success
Test Status: 15/75 passing (baseline maintained)

Known Limitations:
- Dict values in print() still show PyObject struct (needs print enhancement)
- Some edge cases in slicing may need refinement
- Memory leaks in compiler (cosmetic, not runtime)
